selected_important_turn.text: We will likely also need to take water too. Need to be able to wash up.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn escalates competition over water right after the opponent explicitly explained water is critical for them. It shifts the interaction toward a zero-sum contest over the most scarce/high-priority item and narrows options for integrative trades.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Directly contesting an item the opponent reported as critical is likely to provoke resistance and reduces joint value. The turn is a blunt claim rather than a trade proposal or a question to explore whether sharing/splitting is possible. That makes it a harmful turning point with clear room for improvement.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Avoid an outright competing claim. Instead acknowledge the opponent's need and open for trade or splitting (e.g., propose a concrete split, ask whether they absolutely need all the water, or offer to trade extra food for priority on water). Framing as a question or trade preserves cooperative options.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — by opening trade options (e.g., food-for-water) instead of fighting over water, both can get more of what they value.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — a negotiated split or trade is fairer than a unilateral claim that ignores the other's strong need.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — asking/proposing a trade reduces friction and makes agreement more likely than escalation.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase — acknowledging their need and offering trades builds trust rather than antagonism.

value2reason.Low: I already have more than enough food currently.
value2reason.Medium: Firewood will allow me to start a fire and cook my food.
value2reason.High: I need water because it is very hot at my campsite and there is no showers or sinks to wash ourselves with.

value2reason.Low: It will be warm, so a fire is not necessary.
value2reason.Medium: We will need some in case we want to wash up more.
value2reason.High: We are a family of food lovers, always need extra

dialogue.text: Hello, how are you doing today?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: i am doing great! how about you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm doing pretty well, just preparing for this camping trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: ME as well! we are a group of big eaters, and are looking to take lots of food.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: That is fair as there is no showers or sinks at my campsite so I am looking to take water :)
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: We will likely also need to take water too. Need to be able to wash up.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I understand the importance of water for you. How about we split the water 2-1? I can give you 2 water packages and take 1, while we trade some food for better balance.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I appreciate the offer, but I need at least 2 water packages to stay hydrated. How about I take 2 water, 1 firewood, and you take 1 water, 2 firewood, with the remaining food split evenly?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I see your need for water. How about we split the 3 water packages as 2-1, with you taking 2 and me 1? In return, I can offer 1 food package to you, and we can split the remaining
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I need at least 2 water packages to stay hydrated. Let's agree on 2 water for me, 1 for you. In return, I'll take 1 food package and 1 firewood, while you take the remaining 2 food and 2 firewood. That way, we both get what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I think that's a fair split. 2 water, 1 food, and 1 firewood for me, and 1 water, 2 food, and 2 firewood for you. Let's confirm this deal.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
